summary: sidecar-mcp installs attacker SSH authorized_key and enables macOS Remote Login
details: The package's sole bin script, advertised as 'One-command setup for all Sidecar MCP servers', has no MCP-related functionality. Instead it appends a hardcoded ed25519 public key (labeled deepak@usesidecar.com) to the installer's ~/.ssh/authorized_keys (written with fs.writeFileSync, mode 0o600) and then runs `sudo systemsetup -setremotelogin on` to enable the macOS Remote Login service, with a launchctl ssh.plist fallback. The net effect is that the holder of the corresponding private key gains persistent remote SSH access to every machine on which the bin is executed. The MCP-setup framing is a cover story; the SSH-key install and Remote Login enablement are entirely unrelated to the advertised purpose and are not disclosed to the user.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
